title: Assumptions of Linear Regression
concept: Linear regression works best when the relationship is linear, errors are random, and data points are independent.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What does a positive residual tell you about a prediction?

options.text: The actual value was higher than the prediction (the model underpredicted).
The actual value was lower than the prediction (the model overpredicted).
The model made a perfect prediction with zero error.

exercises.explain: Correct! A positive residual means the actual value is greater than the predicted value.

cards.title: Using the right tool
cards.body: You wouldn't use a hammer to wash your windows. Similarly, linear regression is a powerful tool, but it only works correctly when your data fits specific, simple criteria.

cards.title: Key assumptions
cards.body: Linear regression relies on core assumptions: first, the true relationship must be linear (a straight line). Second, the residuals (errors) should be random and independent of each other.

cards.title: Measuring a curved wall
cards.body: Imagine trying to measure the length of a curved brick wall using a stiff wooden ruler. Because the ruler cannot bend, your straight measurement will fail to capture the true shape of the curve.

cards.title: Breaking the rules
cards.body: If you try to model how bacteria grow over time using linear regression, you'll fail. Bacteria grow exponentially (curving upward), so a straight line will predict poorly as time goes on.

cards.title: Errors shouldn't show patterns
cards.body: You might think it's fine if your prediction errors get larger as your inputs grow. However, this pattern (called heteroscedasticity) violates regression assumptions, indicating your model is missing a key factor.

left.text:
Points follow a straight trend
Errors are random
Perfect for Linear Regression

right.text:
Points bend or curve
Errors show a clear shape
Linear Regression fails

exercises.prompt: What is the most fundamental assumption of linear regression?

options.text: The relationship between input and output must be roughly linear (a straight line)
The dataset must contain at least one million rows
All inputs must be negative numbers
The output must always be a category

exercises.explain: Linear regression fits a straight line, so it assumes the true relationship in the data is also straight.

exercises.prompt: True or false: If your model's prediction errors show a clear, non-random pattern, it violates linear regression assumptions.
exercises.explain: True. Residuals should be random 'noise'. If they have a pattern, it means the model is failing to capture a systematic relationship.

exercises.sentence: Linear regression will fail on exponential growth because the relationship is ___.
exercises.explain: Exponential growth curves upward, violating the linearity assumption of linear regression.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following datasets is suitable for simple linear regression?

options.text: Height vs. Age in children (where height increases steadily with age)
Radioactive decay over time (which curves sharply downward)
Day of the week vs. favorite color (categorical labels)

exercises.explain: Steady, straight-line growth like child height vs. age is a great fit for linear regression.

exercises.prompt: You are building a linear regression model to predict ice cream sales based on temperature. However, you notice that sales grow exponentially on hot days, rather than in a straight line. What assumption is violated, and what is the risk?

options.text: Linearity; the model will make poor predictions because a straight line cannot fit a curved pattern.
Independence; the sales on one day depend too much on the previous day.
Homoscedasticity; the variation in sales will become too small on cold days.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Linear regression assumes a straight-line (linear) relationship. If the true pattern is curved, a straight line will consistently underpredict or overpredict at different points. Fantastic job completing the Simple Linear Regression unit!
